review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with Kubernetes security practices related to automated incident handling. However, some improvements in naming consistency and specificity could enhance clarity.
naming_improvements: Ensure 'k8s_admission_security_event_alerting_configured' clearly indicates the specific admission controller or webhook used for alerting.
title: Incident Handling | Automated Incident Handling Processes
step1_reasoning: The control IR-4 (1) focuses on automated incident handling processes. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that security incidents are detected and alerted upon automatically. This involves enabling audit logging to capture security events, configuring admission controllers to alert on security policy violations, and leveraging service mesh capabilities to detect and alert on anomalous traffic patterns. These functions ensure that Kubernetes environments have automated processes to handle security incidents.
